title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## Future New Zealand censuses: Implications of changing census frequency or adopting other models
Informs discussion about the potential for adopting different approaches to census-taking in New Zealand.

## Social and population statistics architecture for New Zealand
Sets out a proposed future direction for social and population statistics in New Zealand across the Official Statistics System.

## A register-based census: what is the potential for New Zealand?
Describes the changes that would be needed in New Zealand to replace the traditional census with a register-based census.

## Demographics of New Zealand’s Pacific Population
Examines the growth of the Pacific population since the 1940s and provides important context for analysing the social and economic position of Pacific peoples.

## Mapping trends in the Auckland region
Explores social, demographic, and economic trends affecting people in the Auckland region, with a focus on geographical patterns and how these are changing over time.

## Māori mobility in New Zealand
Contains data about Māori population movements based on information from the Census of Population and Dwellings 1986–2006.

## Towards a Māori statistics framework
Introduces 2002 work in progress on developing a Māori statistics framework that will align the collecting and reporting of official statistics with Māori issues and concerns.

## Pacific Progress: A Report on the Economic Status of Pacific Peoples in New Zealand
Provides information on the social and economic status of Pacific peoples in New Zealand, based on data from the 2001 Census of Population and Dwellings.

## Review of the measurement of ethnicity: Classifications and issues
Raises some of the classification issues that need to be addressed in the 2001 Review of the Measurement of Ethnicity (RME).

## Adjustment of post-censal population estimates for census undercount
A post-enumeration survey was conducted in New Zealand following the 1996 Census of Population and Dwellings. This report describes and discusses the methodology developed for adjusting the population base.
